events.title: Ringo Starr turns 86
events.blurb: Sir Richard Starkey, known professionally as Ringo Starr, the iconic drummer for The Beatles, was born on July 7, 1940.

angles.name: Ringo's Beat
angles.copy: Eighty-six today for the man who kept the beat for the biggest band in history. Happy Birthday, Ringo Starr!

events.title: Brian May turns 79
events.blurb: Queen's legendary guitarist and astrophysicist, Sir Brian Harold May, was born on July 19, 1947.

angles.name: Guitar God's Birthday
angles.copy: Happy 79th Birthday to the one and only Brian May! Still rocking the Red Special and inspiring generations.

events.title: Mick Jagger turns 83
events.blurb: The charismatic frontman of The Rolling Stones, Sir Michael Philip Jagger, was born on July 26, 1943.

angles.name: Rock Legend's Birthday
angles.copy: Still got the moves at 83! Wishing a very happy birthday to the legendary Mick Jagger.

events.title: MTV launched (1981)
events.blurb: On August 1, 1981, MTV launched with The Buggles' 'Video Killed The Radio Star' as its very first music video.

angles.name: MTV Changed Everything
angles.copy: Forty-five years ago today, MTV changed music forever. Remember the moment 'Video Killed The Radio Star' ushered in a new era?

events.title: Oasis released Definitely Maybe (1994)
events.blurb: Oasis released their groundbreaking debut album 'Definitely Maybe' on August 29, 1994, which would become a cornerstone of Britpop.

angles.name: Definitely Maybe Impact
angles.copy: It's 32 years since Oasis dropped 'Definitely Maybe'. An album that defined a generation and still sounds massive today.

events.title: Woodstock Festival took place (1969)
events.blurb: The legendary Woodstock Music & Art Fair, a pivotal moment in music history, took place from August 15–18, 1969.

angles.name: Peace Love Music
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years ago, half a million people gathered for three days of peace, love, and music. The spirit of Woodstock lives on.

events.title: Elvis Presley would have been 91
events.blurb: Elvis Aaron Presley, the King of Rock and Roll, who passed away on August 16, 1977, would have celebrated his 91st birthday this year, having been born on January 8, 1935.

angles.name: Remembering The King
angles.copy: Remembering the King of Rock and Roll, Elvis Presley, who would have been 91 today. His music truly lives forever.

events.title: The Beatles released Abbey Road (1969)
events.blurb: The Beatles released their iconic album 'Abbey Road' on September 26, 1969, featuring that unforgettable zebra crossing cover.

angles.name: Abbey Road Iconic
angles.copy: Fifty-seven years since The Beatles gave us 'Abbey Road'. Still one of the most recognisable album covers and soundtracks of all time.

events.title: Chrissie Hynde turns 75
events.blurb: Chrissie Hynde, the distinctive voice and frontwoman of The Pretenders, was born on September 7, 1951.

angles.name: Pretenders Frontwoman
angles.copy: Happy 75th Birthday to the legendary Chrissie Hynde! Still captivating audiences with her incredible voice and presence.

events.title: Liam Gallagher turns 54
events.blurb: The outspoken and iconic frontman of Oasis, Liam Gallagher, was born on September 21, 1972.

angles.name: Rock N Roll Star
angles.copy: Happy 54th Birthday to the one and only Liam Gallagher. Still bringing the rock 'n' roll attitude. As you were!

events.title: John Lennon released Imagine (1971)
events.blurb: John Lennon released his timeless anthem 'Imagine' on September 9, 1971, which became a global message of peace and hope.

angles.name: Imagine Peace
angles.copy: Fifty-five years since John Lennon asked us to 'Imagine'. A powerful message that resonates just as strongly today.

events.title: Apple released the first iPod (2001)
events.blurb: On October 23, 2001, Apple introduced the first iPod, revolutionising how we carry and listen to music.

angles.name: iPod Revolution
angles.copy: Remember life before the iPod? Twenty-five years ago, Apple dropped the original, changing portable music forever.

events.title: James Bond Day
events.blurb: October 5th is celebrated annually as James Bond Day, marking the release date of the very first Bond film, 'Dr. No', in 1962.

angles.name: 007 Day
angles.copy: Happy James Bond Day! Grab your martini (shaken, not stirred) and celebrate the world's most famous secret agent.

events.title: John Lennon would have been 86
events.blurb: John Winston Ono Lennon, the legendary singer-songwriter of The Beatles, who was tragically killed on December 8, 1980, would have celebrated his 86th birthday today, having been born on October 9, 1940.

angles.name: Lennon's Legacy
angles.copy: Remembering John Lennon on what would have been his 86th birthday. His music and message continue to inspire.
